Guiding Principles for AI Use in Teaching
Garnet AI Foundry — central AI innovation, collaboration, and support hub

ai_strategy.summary: USC has moved from ad hoc adoption to coordinated strategy in 2024-2026. The 2024-2025 Provost's AI Task Force produced a sector-aligned report that now anchors faculty teaching principles, academic-integrity posture, and enterprise tool selection. In April 2026, Provost Mary Anne Fitzpatrick appointed biomedical engineering chair Mark Uline as USC's first Associate Vice President and Vice Provost for AI Strategy, pairing a new academic strategy office with the Garnet AI Foundry (Division of IT) as the operational hub. Columbia campus strategy emphasizes three pillars: free enterprise AI access for all 40,000+ students, faculty, and staff via ChatGPT Edu and discounted Microsoft 365 Copilot; AI literacy for every undergraduate major via a new interdisciplinary certificate; and a research engine anchored by the AI Institute (AIISC), Theia AI cluster, and ASPIRE AI internal seed program.

key_provisions: Faculty must state in the syllabus whether AI use is allowed or prohibited in each course; AI use falls under the Honor Code (cheating, plagiarism, falsification, or complicity) when misused

Seven CTE guiding principles: support (not replace) teaching, clear syllabus expectations, faculty transparency, verify AI outputs, protect student privacy, promote critical AI awareness, build faculty AI literacy

ChatGPT Edu (via OpenAI) is FERPA-covered through a Student Data Privacy Agreement designating OpenAI a 'school official'; ChatGPT Edu does NOT support regulated research, PII, HIPAA, or FERPA information directly — Microsoft Copilot is the only approved tool for regulated/PII/HIPAA/FERPA data

Students must cite and disclose any permitted AI use in submitted work; first-time violations typically receive six months of conduct probation plus the AI-focused educational module or Academic Integrity Workshop
Conversations and files in USC-licensed AI tools are NOT used to train external models and are private within USC's environment; USC IT does not access chat history
AI use is expected to align with transparency, fairness, and accuracy principles including disclosure to students, avoidance of overreliance in grading, and protection of student data
ai_policy.summary: USC operates a comprehensive, layered AI policy: the Provost's 2024-2025 AI Task Force Report drives institutional direction; the Center for Teaching Excellence's Guiding Principles (published Nov. 11, 2025) govern teaching use with a mandatory syllabus-disclosure expectation; Student Conduct maps AI misuse to existing Honor Code violations; and the Division of IT publishes a tiered approved-tools catalog with explicit data-class restrictions (ChatGPT Edu for non-regulated work, Microsoft Copilot for regulated/PII/FERPA/HIPAA).

bodies.name: Office of the Associate Vice President and Vice Provost for AI Strategy
bodies.scope: University-wide academic AI strategy across research, teaching, student experience, and cross-college collaboration

bodies.name: Provost's Task Force on the Use of Artificial Intelligence (2024-2025)
bodies.scope: Developed comprehensive research-based guidelines for faculty on AI in teaching and learning (academic integrity, privacy, accessibility, pedagogy); co-chaired by Dr. Lara Lomicka Anderson and Dr. Aaron Marterer

bodies.name: Garnet AI Foundry (Division of Information Technology)
bodies.scope: Central hub for AI innovation, tools, training, governance, and support across Columbia campus; houses ChatGPT Edu, Microsoft Copilot, Garnet & Black GPT collection, prompt library, AI Educators team, and Aila virtual tutor

bodies.name: Center for Teaching Excellence — GenAI Program
bodies.scope: Faculty development on AI in teaching, self-paced Teaching with AI course, GenAI Community of Practice, Teaching and Learning with GenAI Certificate

bodies.name: Artificial Intelligence Institute of South Carolina (AIISC)
bodies.scope: University-wide AI research excellence initiative; neuro-symbolic AI, knowledge graphs, health informatics; founded July 2019 with $5M commitment ($1M/year for five years)

bodies.name: AI Committee (Division of IT cross-campus)
bodies.scope: Cross-campus collaboration on AI tool selection, rollout, and governance referenced in Microsoft case study

governance_structure.summary: USC's governance stack pairs an academic-side office (the new Vice Provost for AI Strategy, April 2026) with an IT-side operational hub (Garnet AI Foundry) and a research institute (AIISC). The 2024-2025 Provost's Task Force transitioned from advisory body to implemented policy through the CTE Guiding Principles and the Vice Provost role, which is staffed by a task-force alumnus. This mirrors the bifurcated chief-AI-officer / CAIO-plus-CIO model emerging at peer R1s, but routed through the Provost rather than the health system.

initiatives.title: ChatGPT Edu campus-wide deployment via OpenAI partnership
initiatives.description: USC contracted with OpenAI to provide free ChatGPT Edu access to every student, faculty, and staff member on the Columbia campus. Over 23,000 active users were reported at the Garnet AI Foundry hub within months of launch, making USC one of the earliest and largest R1 deployments of ChatGPT Edu.
initiatives.significance: Sets a public flagship precedent for giving 40,000+ users free, FERPA-covered access to a frontier LLM as a tuition-adjacent benefit — and does so from a state without a finalized statewide higher-ed AI framework.

investment.funding_source: University operating budget (replaces per-user consumer subscriptions)
investment.description: Enterprise ChatGPT Edu license covering all 40,000+ Columbia-campus users; specific contract value not disclosed

collaborations.description: OpenAI signed a Student Data Privacy Agreement designating itself a FERPA 'school official' for USC users

tools_and_vendors.name: ChatGPT Edu
tools_and_vendors.role: Campus-wide generative AI assistant for learning, research, and administrative work

initiatives.policy_implications: FERPA alignment achieved via Student Data Privacy Agreement; PII/HIPAA/regulated-research use explicitly routed to Microsoft Copilot instead

initiatives.title: Microsoft 365 Copilot pilot for regulated-data AI workflows
initiatives.description: USC deployed Microsoft 365 Copilot alongside ChatGPT Edu, positioning it as the university's approved AI tool for regulated research, PII, HIPAA, and FERPA-covered work. Faculty and staff can purchase licenses for $250/year; a self-reported 84% of pilot users saved 1-5 hours per week on drafting research papers, meeting notes, procurement reviews, and IT service-desk responses.
initiatives.significance: Demonstrates the emerging two-tool R1 pattern — an OpenAI offering for general productivity and a Microsoft offering for data-sensitive workflows — that resolves the ChatGPT-PII/FERPA gap without banning either vendor.

investment.funding_source: $250/user/year self-funded faculty and staff plus departmental budgets
investment.description: Microsoft 365 Copilot licenses at $250/user/year; pilot scope growing through AI Committee guidance

collaborations.description: Microsoft customer case study featuring faculty and IT leadership; cross-campus AI Committee coordinates rollout

tools_and_vendors.name: Microsoft 365 Copilot
tools_and_vendors.role: Enterprise AI assistant integrated across Word, Teams, Outlook for regulated-data workflows

initiatives.policy_implications: USC policy explicitly limits ChatGPT Edu to non-regulated data and routes HIPAA/FERPA/PII/research-regulated AI use to Copilot — an unusually explicit two-tier data-class rule

initiatives.title: Vice Provost for AI Strategy (first in USC history)
initiatives.description: On April 10, 2026, Provost Mary Anne Fitzpatrick appointed Mark Uline — chair of biomedical engineering and a 2024-25 Provost's AI Task Force member — as USC's first Associate Vice President and Vice Provost for AI Strategy. The role advances AI in research, scholarship, and creative arts; enhances student experience; and coordinates across colleges balancing innovation with integrity.
initiatives.significance: Creation of a dedicated vice-provost-level AI role at a public flagship signals AI has shifted from IT project to enterprise academic strategy, following peer moves at Miami, Minnesota, and other R1s.

investment.description: New administrative role; compensation and office size not publicly disclosed

initiatives.policy_implications: Centralizes academic AI strategy in the Provost's office, complementing the Division of IT's Garnet AI Foundry operational hub

initiatives.title: Garnet AI Foundry launch
initiatives.description: Division of IT launched the Garnet AI Foundry as USC's central AI hub, housing ChatGPT Edu access, a Garnet & Black custom GPT collection, a prompt library, role-specific resources for students/faculty/staff, the AI Educators team, the Garnet AI Fluency 101 course with the 'Aila' 24/7 virtual tutor, and the annual AI Day at USC event.
initiatives.significance: A unified campus AI portal — not just a policy page — is becoming the default R1 infrastructure pattern for managing tool sprawl, custom GPTs, and training at scale.

investment.description: Central portal, custom-GPT hosting, training team, and annual event; specific budget not disclosed

collaborations.description: Vendor ecosystem anchoring the AI Day keynote and campus-wide tools

tools_and_vendors.name: Garnet AI Foundry Portal
tools_and_vendors.role: Central hub aggregating tools, prompts, custom GPTs, and training

tools_and_vendors.name: Aila AI Fluency Tutor
tools_and_vendors.role: 24/7 virtual tutor for Garnet AI Fluency 101 course

tools_and_vendors.name: Gartner Campus Access
tools_and_vendors.role: Research and analysis platform integrated into the AI Foundry resource set

initiatives.policy_implications: Gives USC a single front door for AI governance enforcement — approved tools, prompt guidance, and training are all routed through one portal

initiatives.title: AI Day at USC statewide event (inaugural, March 2026)
initiatives.description: The Garnet AI Foundry hosted the inaugural AI Day at USC on March 30, 2026, at Russell House, drawing over 700 attendees from USC, partner institutions across SC/NC/GA, with 14 breakout sessions, 35 poster presentations, and two panels. Attendee mix: 47.5% staff, 19.8% faculty, 13% graduate students, with more than half self-identifying as AI beginners.
initiatives.significance: Positions USC as a regional convener for AI in higher education, extending reach beyond Columbia to the Southeast corridor and setting precedent for annual state-level AI-in-HE gatherings.

investment.description: One-day event with sponsor support; budget not disclosed

partners: Regional institutions in SC, NC, and GA

initiatives.title: AI Literacy Interdisciplinary Certificate for undergraduates
initiatives.description: Launched fall 2025 and administered through the Provost's Office of Undergraduate Studies, USC's AI Literacy Certificate is open to students in all majors. The 12-credit-minimum program consists of two required courses on AI capabilities and ethics plus two electives tying AI to the student's major. Outcomes include explaining key AI algorithms, recognizing ethical implications, and evaluating bias, privacy, transparency, and accountability issues.
initiatives.significance: Moves AI literacy from an optional CS course to a cross-disciplinary credential available to every undergraduate — a concrete move toward the 'AI-for-all-majors' model many public flagships are only discussing.

investment.funding_source: Office of the Provost and participating colleges
investment.description: Curriculum development and instructional capacity across participating departments

initiatives.policy_implications: Institutionalizes AI literacy as a general-education pathway available to any major

initiatives.title: AI Institute of South Carolina (AIISC) research excellence initiative
initiatives.description: USC committed $1M/year for five years ($5M total) in 2019 to stand up the university-wide AI Institute, recruited Prof. Amit Sheth as founding director (NCR Chair), and renovated the former law center on South Main Street to house the institute. Research focus: neuro-symbolic AI, knowledge graphs, knowledge-infused learning, NLP, computer vision, and health informatics. Core faculty of ~9 plus 3 staff scientists; advisory board spans School of Medicine, Arnold School of Public Health, and engineering.
initiatives.significance: AIISC gave USC one of the earlier university-wide AI institutes in the Southeast and established the neuro-symbolic / knowledge-graph research niche that now differentiates it from LLM-only peers.

investment.description: $1M/year for five years to establish the university-wide AI Institute and renovate physical space

partners: Molinaroli College of Engineering and Computing

collaborations.description: Translational AI work with industry sponsors

initiatives.title: NSF MRI grant for AI-for-science HPC cluster (Theia)
initiatives.description: In September 2023 USC received a $1.1M NSF Major Research Instrumentation (MRI) award (PI Ming Hu, Mechanical Engineering) to build an AI-focused HPC cluster with GPU and CPU capacity. The Theia cluster — 28 112-core CPU nodes, 10 quad A100/H100 GPU nodes, 3,808 cores, 675 TFLOPS peak, 1.5 PB GPFS scratch — is now in early-stage production alongside the flagship Hyperion cluster (16,616 cores, 1.5 PFlops). Access extends to 10+ regional universities including Claflin, Furman, Francis Marion, Coastal Carolina, and College of Charleston.
initiatives.significance: Pairs a federally-funded AI-optimized research cluster with a regional-access mission, positioning USC as a state HPC anchor for minority-serving and smaller institutions.

investment.funding_source: National Science Foundation Major Research Instrumentation (MRI) program
investment.description: $1.1M federal instrumentation grant for AI-focused HPC cluster with regional multi-institution access

collaborations.description: Shared access to Theia cluster for research and training

initiatives.title: ASPIRE AI internal faculty seed program
initiatives.description: USC's Office of the Vice President for Research operates ASPIRE AI, an internal funding program targeting faculty who intend to pursue extramural AI funding, with special focus on interdisciplinary teams pairing AI expertise with domain knowledge. The 2025-26 cohort supports 23 individual faculty scholarly projects plus 3 faculty team projects; the next deadline is February 18, 2026.
initiatives.significance: Internal seed funding aimed specifically at AI — rather than generic research stimulus — is an emerging R1 lever for preparing competitive federal AI-institute and industry-partnership proposals.

investment.funding_source: Office of the Vice President for Research (internal)
investment.description: Seed awards to ~26 faculty/team projects per cycle to prepare competitive extramural AI proposals; individual award size not publicly disclosed

initiatives.title: AI-4-SC partnership — Palmetto College + College of Information and Communications
initiatives.description: In October 2025, USC Palmetto College (the multi-campus regional system) and the College of Information and Communications formalized a partnership to bring AI education and workforce readiness to South Carolinians statewide through a network of innovation labs. Initial offerings include two 90-minute interactive seminars — Generative AI for Beginners and Utilizing AI for Your Business — targeted at adult learners, job seekers, and non-technical residents, with a free public completion certificate.
initiatives.significance: Extends AI access beyond degree-seeking students into adult workforce and underserved communities, using the regional Palmetto College network to deliver training across small-town and rural SC.

investment.funding_source: USC Palmetto College, CIC, and state grant support for citizen AI training
investment.description: Free public seminars, innovation-lab network across regional campuses

partners: State of South Carolina (small business / citizen training grant)

tools_and_vendors.name: Grow with Google AI training
tools_and_vendors.role: Partner curriculum for small-business and nonprofit AI training

initiatives.title: Graduate AI Certificate (Molinaroli College of Engineering and Computing)
initiatives.description: The Molinaroli College offers a 12-credit-hour Artificial Intelligence Graduate Certificate covering knowledge representation and reasoning, computer vision, NLP, reasoning under uncertainty, and machine learning. It can be pursued independently or concurrently with other graduate programs; qualified undergraduates may stack it via the Accelerated Bachelor's/Graduate plan.
initiatives.significance: Provides a stackable AI credential for working professionals and accelerated students while staying anchored to AIISC research capacity.

initiatives.title: Teaching and Learning with GenAI Certificate for faculty (Center for Teaching Excellence)
initiatives.description: The CTE runs a cohorted Teaching and Learning with GenAI Certificate program; the fall 2025 cohort completed the program and was recognized in early 2026. Complemented by a self-paced Teaching with AI course, GenAI Community of Practice, and workshops — with 30 training sessions through March 2025 generating 440 registrations and 247 attendees (faculty, staff, graduate, and undergraduate students).
initiatives.significance: Professional-development credentialing for faculty is a leading indicator that AI pedagogy training is shifting from webinar culture to sustained certificate programs.

investment.funding_source: Center for Teaching Excellence operating budget
investment.description: Certificate cohorts, self-paced course, workshops, community of practice

initiatives.policy_implications: Operationalizes the CTE Guiding Principles by training faculty in syllabus-level policy design and classroom practice

initiatives.title: CTE Guiding Principles for AI Use in Teaching
initiatives.description: Published November 11, 2025 by the Center for Teaching Excellence (author Aisha Haynes), aligned with the Provost's 2024-25 AI Task Force Report. Seven principles: support (don't replace) teaching; communicate syllabus expectations; be transparent about faculty AI use; verify AI-generated content; protect student privacy; promote critical AI awareness in students; build faculty AI literacy. Pairs with mandated syllabus disclosure of AI allowances.
initiatives.significance: One of a handful of R1 'principles + syllabus mandate' frameworks that combine faculty autonomy with institutional transparency requirements.
initiatives.policy_implications: Requires syllabus-level disclosure as a default expectation for all USC courses

initiatives.title: Academic Integrity framework mapping AI misuse to the Honor Code
initiatives.description: The Office of Student Conduct and Academic Integrity treats AI-related misconduct as existing Honor Code violations (cheating, plagiarism, falsification, complicity). Students must obtain instructor permission before using AI and must disclose permitted use in all submissions. In the 2024-25 academic year, 34% of all Honor Code cases referenced AI use — up sharply from the prior year. First-time violators typically receive six months of conduct probation plus completion of either the Academic Integrity Workshop or an AI-focused educational module.
initiatives.significance: The jump to 34% of honor-code cases involving AI — up from a much smaller share — is a concrete sector signal that enforcement has moved from theoretical to operational at R1 scale.

investment.funding_source: Office of Student Conduct and Academic Integrity

initiatives.policy_implications: Maps AI-specific misuse onto existing Honor Code categories rather than creating a standalone AI conduct code

initiatives.title: USC Aiken 'Ace' admissions chatbot (satellite campus)
initiatives.description: USC Aiken (the R1 system's four-year branch campus) partnered with AdmitHub (now Mainstay) in 2019 to launch 'Ace,' a conversational AI chatbot accessible via SMS and Facebook Messenger for prospective-student admissions support. Post-launch results: 28% increase in enrollment commitments, 58% increase in first-year orientation signups (fall 2018 vs. fall 2019), and 200+ staff hours saved.
initiatives.significance: One of the earlier SEC/Carolinas chatbot deployments with documented melt-reduction outcomes; foreshadows the current wave of R1 admissions AI and illustrates that branch campuses often lead on student-service AI.

initiatives.title: AI in Action summit (Office of Research + Darla Moore School of Business)
initiatives.description: The Office of the Vice President for Research and the Darla Moore School of Business co-hosted the AI in Action summit April 16-17, 2026 at Gateway 737 in Columbia. Agenda: keynote, industry panels in energy / healthcare / risk-and-insurance, and breakout sessions on building education and R&D partnerships. Focus on how AI reshapes SC-economy sectors and the workforce skills universities must develop.
initiatives.significance: Pairs a research-office convening with a business school to fuse industry/academic partnership-building with AI strategy — a model for translating state-economy needs into university programming.

investment.funding_source: Office of the Vice President for Research and Darla Moore School

partners: Regional industry executives across energy, healthcare, risk/insurance
collaborations.description: Cross-sector R&D and education partnership development

initiatives.title: ChatGPT Edu 23,000 active-user deployment
initiatives.description: USC reports ChatGPT Edu as a university partnership with OpenAI and notes 23,000 active users, making it one of the clearer public platform-usage disclosures in this sweep.
initiatives.significance: The active-user count directly addresses the common platform-usage gap.

investment.description: USC publishes active-user count but not ChatGPT Edu contract cost.

collaborations.description: USC announced a university partnership with OpenAI around ChatGPT Edu.

initiatives.policy_implications: Campus deployment is tied to university AI governance and CTE teaching principles.

computing_infrastructure.summary: USC operates two major HPC environments through its Division of IT Research Computing group. Hyperion Phase III is the flagship: 356 compute/GPU/Big Memory nodes, 16,616 CPU cores, 1.5 PFlops peak, EDR Infiniband (100 Gb/s), 1.4 PB GPFS scratch. Theia is the newer AI-focused cluster: 28 112-core CPU nodes, 10 quad-GPU nodes with NVIDIA A100 and H100 cards (approx. 40 GPUs), 3,808 cores, 675 TFLOPS peak, 1.5 PB GPFS scratch — funded in part by a $1.1M NSF MRI grant. The AIISC also maintains its own deep-learning GPU resources including 8 A100 'Multi-Instance GPU' (usable as 56 GPUs) with 1 TB RAM. USC instructors have free access to the University of Florida HiPerGator cluster for teaching.

regulatory_context.summary: As of April 2026, South Carolina has no enacted statewide higher-ed AI law. The state legislature is considering H.B. 4582 (authorizing age-appropriate AI instruction in K-12 beginning 2026-2027) and H.B. 5253 (requiring parental opt-in consent and annual AI-tool disclosure, prohibiting AI from replacing licensed teachers or grading work in K-12) — both K-12-focused. The SC Department of Education published an AI framework in March 2023. Because South Carolina has no unified higher-ed system governing authority over the separately-governed USC system, USC, Clemson, Medical University of South Carolina, and other institutions set their own AI policies. USC's policies are therefore not constrained by a system-wide framework.

controversies_and_litigation.summary: Jump in Honor Code cases referencing AI: 34% of all 2024-25 USC Honor Code cases involved AI use, up sharply from the prior year, prompting expansion of educational modules. Not a lawsuit, but a notable enforcement signal.

narrative_summary: The University of South Carolina is a public R1 flagship (40,000+ Columbia-campus enrollment) executing one of the more coordinated AI strategies in the Southeast. Research leadership came first — the AI Institute (AIISC) launched in 2019 with a $5M commitment and founding director Amit Sheth built a national neuro-symbolic AI and knowledge-graph niche — followed by computing infrastructure in 2023 (NSF MRI-funded Theia cluster with regional multi-institution access, alongside flagship Hyperion HPC). The 2024-2025 wave shifted to enterprise and academic strategy. Under President Michael Amiridis and new Provost Mary Anne Fitzpatrick, USC stood up a full consumer-scale enterprise AI stack: free ChatGPT Edu for all 40,000+ users, Microsoft 365 Copilot for regulated-data workflows ($250/user/year), the Garnet AI Foundry as a unified campus portal, 14 breakout sessions at a 700-attendee inaugural AI Day, and an interdisciplinary AI Literacy Certificate open to every undergraduate major. Academic governance caught up in April 2026 with the appointment of Mark Uline as USC's first Associate Vice President and Vice Provost for AI Strategy — pairing a Provost's-office AI lead with the IT-side Garnet AI Foundry operational hub. Policy is comprehensive and layered: the Provost's 2024-25 AI Task Force Report drives strategy; CTE Guiding Principles (Nov 2025) require syllabus-level disclosure; Student Conduct routes AI misuse through the existing Honor Code (34% of 2024-25 cases referenced AI); Division of IT enforces a two-tier data-class rule (ChatGPT Edu for non-regulated, Microsoft Copilot for FERPA/HIPAA/PII/regulated research). Workforce and equity pillars round out the posture: the AI-4-SC partnership with Palmetto College extends free AI training to adult learners statewide, Theia compute serves 10+ regional universities including the HBCUs Benedict and Claflin, and the Darla Moore School's AI in Action summit connects industry to the SC economy. Gaps: no publicly disclosed total enterprise AI spending, no system-wide SC AI policy to anchor to, no disclosed chief-AI-officer title (the new role is vice-provost-scoped with a strong academic bias), and no public dashboard of AI-project counts comparable to Miami or Minnesota peers.

gaps_acknowledged: Full text of the 2024-2025 Provost's AI Task Force Report was a PDF that the web-fetch step could not parse; only the CTE-published seven-principle summary is captured here

The USC System's branch-campus AI posture (USC Aiken, USC Upstate, USC Beaufort, USC Lancaster, USC Sumter, USC Salkehatchie, USC Union) is mentioned only for the 2019 USC Aiken admissions chatbot and the Palmetto College AI-4-SC seminars — deeper per-campus profiles would require more research
No publicly announced standalone AI research gift from an external donor (beyond internal university commitment and NSF MRI grant)

Predictive-analytics-retention tools and mental-health AI at the student-services level were not surfaced in the public-web search despite a 40K-student enrollment; likely underreported rather than absent

University of South Carolina (internally built) Garnet AI Foundry Portal

public_claims.title: ChatGPT Edu campus-wide deployment via OpenAI partnership
public_claims.summary: Sets a public flagship precedent for giving 40,000+ users free, FERPA-covered access to a frontier LLM as a tuition-adjacent benefit — and does so from a state without a finalized statewide higher-ed AI framework.

public_claims.title: Microsoft 365 Copilot pilot for regulated-data AI workflows
public_claims.summary: Demonstrates the emerging two-tool R1 pattern — an OpenAI offering for general productivity and a Microsoft offering for data-sensitive workflows — that resolves the ChatGPT-PII/FERPA gap without banning either vendor.

public_claims.title: AI-4-SC partnership — Palmetto College + College of Information and Communications
public_claims.summary: Extends AI access beyond degree-seeking students into adult workforce and underserved communities, using the regional Palmetto College network to deliver training across small-town and rural SC.

public_claims.title: Academic Integrity framework mapping AI misuse to the Honor Code
public_claims.summary: The jump to 34% of honor-code cases involving AI — up from a much smaller share — is a concrete sector signal that enforcement has moved from theoretical to operational at R1 scale.

public_claims.title: USC Aiken 'Ace' admissions chatbot (satellite campus)
public_claims.summary: One of the earlier SEC/Carolinas chatbot deployments with documented melt-reduction outcomes; foreshadows the current wave of R1 admissions AI and illustrates that branch campuses often lead on student-service AI.

public_claims.title: AI in Action summit (Office of Research + Darla Moore School of Business)
public_claims.summary: Pairs a research-office convening with a business school to fuse industry/academic partnership-building with AI strategy — a model for translating state-economy needs into university programming.

public_claims.title: ChatGPT Edu 23,000 active-user deployment
public_claims.summary: The active-user count directly addresses the common platform-usage gap.

risk_readiness.rationale: Risk-bearing AI signals are paired with multiple public policy/control indicators.

notes: Full text of the 2024-2025 Provost's AI Task Force Report was a PDF that the web-fetch step could not parse; only the CTE-published seven-principle summary is captured here
No public disclosure of the total enterprise spend on ChatGPT Edu or Microsoft 365 Copilot contracts

No identified Chief AI Officer title distinct from the Vice Provost for AI Strategy role (Mark Uline); the health/clinical side is separately run through the Medical University of South Carolina (MUSC), which is a separate institution
ASPIRE AI per-award dollar amount is not publicly disclosed

The USC System's branch-campus AI posture (USC Aiken, USC Upstate, USC Beaufort, USC Lancaster, USC Sumter, USC Salkehatchie, USC Union) is mentioned only for the 2019 USC Aiken admissions chatbot and the Palmetto College AI-4-SC seminars — deeper per-campus profiles would require more research
No publicly announced standalone AI research gift from an external donor (beyond internal university commitment and NSF MRI grant)

Predictive-analytics-retention tools and mental-health AI at the student-services level were not surfaced in the public-web search despite a 40K-student enrollment; likely underreported rather than absent
USC publishes a 23,000 active-user ChatGPT Edu signal, but contract cost and Microsoft Copilot license count remain undisclosed.
